fresh_facts.claim: Approximately 2,500 U.S. Marines from the 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit and the amphibious assault ship USS Tripoli deployed to the Middle East region, arriving over a week away from Iranian waters.
Israel conducted approximately 7,600 airstrikes over two weeks, including 2,000+ on Iranian regime headquarters and assets, and 4,700 on missile programs.
U.S. military struck more than 15,000 targets in Iran over nearly two weeks, according to Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.
President Trump announced U.S. bombing obliterated every military target on Kharg Island, Iran's key oil export hub, while sparing oil infrastructure.
13 U.S. military personnel were killed as of March 13, 2026, and 4 U.S. soldiers died in an aircraft collision in Iraq on March 14.
Israeli Defense Force estimates 4,000-5,000 Iranian soldiers and 380 Hezbollah terrorists neutralized.
Approximately 90+ Iranian vessels have been sunk during the conflict.
Iran experienced a 95% drop in drone attacks since the war began.
Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf warned on March 12, 2026, of abandoning all restraint if southern Gulf islands such as Kharg are attacked.
Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first public statement as Iran's new Supreme Leader on March 13, 2026, vowing to continue blocking the Strait of Hormuz and calling for closing U.S. bases.
Ali Khamenei, Iran's previous Supreme Leader, was assassinated on February 28, 2026, along with family members.
Hardliners criticized President Masoud Pezeshkian's regrets over Iranian regional attacks on March 7, 2026.
Israeli PM Netanyahu vowed on March 13, 2026, continued attacks on Iran and Lebanon, threatening Mojtaba Khamenei.
Israeli and Iranian envoys clashed at the UN Security Council on March 11, 2026, with Israel accusing Iran of civilian targeting and nuclear pursuits.
U.S. and Israeli strikes targeted IRGC infrastructure in Tehran on March 7, 2026.
Vice President Vance reported on March 13, 2026, a possible injury to Iran's new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei from a U.S. or Israeli strike.
The most recent indirect U.S.-Iran talks occurred on February 26, 2026, in Geneva, mediated by Oman, focusing on Iran's nuclear program.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei rejected U.S. proposals on February 17, 2026.
A U.S.-Iran negotiation round in Muscat ended without breakthroughs on February 6, 2026.
Trump noted on Fox News Radio on March 13, 2026, that Basij and machine-gun-equipped forces are suppressing Iranian protesters.
Iranian and Hezbollah missile attacks continue from Lebanon against Israeli cities including Jerusalem, Tel Aviv, and Eilat.
Approximately 5,000 U.S. forces were already deployed in the region prior to the additional Marine deployment.

regime_fall.current_status: Unprecedented military escalation continues with combined US (15,000+ strikes), Israeli (7,600 strikes), and regional operations destroying Iranian military infrastructure, naval assets, and IRGC capabilities, while Mojtaba Khamenei consolidates power as new Supreme Leader amid reported possible injury, internal hardliner tensions, and ongoing coordinated Iranian/Hezbollah missile attacks despite 95% reduction in drone capacity.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Israeli strikes targeted Iranian regime headquarters and assets with 7,600 total airstrikes over two weeks while US conducted 15,000+ strikes, destroying Kharg Island military targets and sinking 90+ Iranian vessels, as Mojtaba Khamenei issued first public statement as Supreme Leader vowing to continue Strait of Hormuz blockade.
